# Lines, Circles & Linear Modeling

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this chapter, you will be able to:

- Write and interpret the equation of a line in slope-intercept and point-slope form.
- Write and interpret the equation of a circle.
- Fit a linear model to environmental data and interpret its slope and intercept.
- Solve for the intersection of two lines and interpret the result in context.
- Model uniform linear motion using parametric equations.

---

## Equations of Lines

*(Slope-intercept form, point-slope form; interpreting slope as a rate.)*

## Circles

*(Equation of a circle; center/radius; buffer zones around a contamination source.)*

### Concept Check: Circle Equations {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Linear Modeling from Data

*(Fitting a line to real data — e.g. long-term CO2 or temperature trend.)*

## Intersections of Lines

*(Solving systems of linear equations; interpreting the intersection point.)*

### Concept Check: Interpreting Slope {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Uniform Linear Motion & Parametric Equations

*(Two moving point-sources modeled parametrically; where/when their paths cross.)*

## Practice: Fit-a-Line Case Study

*(To be drafted.)*

## Skills Drills

### Writing Line Equations

*(To be drafted.)*

### Solving Intersections Algebraically

*(To be drafted.)*
